preview_text:
The model favors Ecuador at 82%, assigning Curaçao only a 2% chance of victory. This reflects a stark asymmetry: Ecuador enters as a heavy favorite, while an upset ranks among the least likely outcomes in the forecast set.

The spread across outcomes—win, draw, loss—reveals a match tilted decisively toward one competitor. A draw sits at 17%, making it the only realistic alternative to an Ecuador win. Curaçao's path to points is narrow; the model estimates the contest will be competitive in structure but lopsided in likely outcome.

The sportsbook consensus diverges from the model on Curaçao's prospects. Books assign the underdog 8%, a gap of 6 percentage points above the model's 2%. Ecuador's win probability aligns more closely, with the market at 76% versus the model at 82%—a 6-point difference in the opposite direction. The dashboard does not adjudicate between these views; both reflect legitimate inference from incomplete information.
